title: Add coupon discount support to charge endpoint
summary: This PR introduces coupon discount support but has two blocking bugs (a PCI data leak via a debug console.log and a logic error that makes discount_amount always 0), plus several quality issues: silent exception swallowing, the possibility of producing a negative charge amount on small orders, snake_case response fields that break the rest of the API's camelCase convention, type erasure via `any`, leftover debug identifiers, and use of `var`/`==` that contradict the surrounding TypeScript style.

findings.title: DEBUG console.log dumps raw card data including PAN and CVC
findings.message: Line 16 serialises the entire raw request body—including `card.number` and `card.cvc`—and writes it to stdout via `console.log`. The project's pino logger (`src/logger.ts`) has explicit redact paths for `card.number` and `card.cvc` specifically to prevent this. Bypassing it constitutes a PCI DSS data leak; any log aggregator (Datadog, CloudWatch, etc.) will store the plain-text card number. Remove this line entirely or, if request tracing is needed, use `logger.debug({ requestId: req.id }, 'charge_request_received')` with no body payload.

findings.title: discount_amount is always 0 — payload.amount is overwritten before the diff
findings.message: Line 23 writes `payload.amount = finalAmount`. Then line 35 computes `payload.amount - finalAmount`, which is now always `0`. The discount is silently thrown away and clients can never see the savings the PR promises. Fix: capture the original amount before applying the coupon, e.g. `const originalAmount = payload.amount;` and then compute `response.discountAmount = originalAmount - finalAmount`.

findings.title: Inner try/catch silently swallows all coupon errors and charges original amount without notice
findings.message: The empty `catch (e) { // TODO }` block means any exception from `doStuff` is suppressed. The charge then proceeds at the undiscounted amount, but the response still includes `coupon_code`, giving the caller the false impression the coupon was honoured. At minimum, log the error and return a `400 invalid_coupon_code` response (or rethrow). Silent failure with misleading output is worse than a visible error.

findings.title: FixedCouponStrategy can produce a negative amount, causing a 500 on small orders
findings.message: `apply` returns `amount - this.amt` with no floor check. If a customer applies `FIVEOFF` (500 cents) to a $3.00 charge (300 cents), the result is -200. That negative value reaches `paymentsService.assertPositiveAmount`, which throws a plain `Error` (not an `AppError`). The error handler returns a 500 rather than a meaningful 4xx. Fix: add `return Math.max(0, amount - this.amt)` or throw an `AppError('validation_error', ...)` when the discount would exceed the charge.

findings.title: Response fields use snake_case while the rest of the API uses camelCase
findings.message: All existing `Transaction` fields (`cardLast4`, `customerId`, `gatewayReference`, `createdAt`) are camelCase. The new fields `discount_amount` and `coupon_code` break that contract. Rename them to `discountAmount` and `couponCode`. Also, casting `transaction` to `any` erases type safety; define a proper response type (e.g. `type ChargeResponse = Transaction & { discountAmount: number; couponCode?: string }`) instead.

findings.title: console.log in couponDiscount instead of the project's pino logger
findings.message: All other logging in the service (paymentsService, errorHandler, index) uses the shared `logger` from `src/logger.ts`. This `console.log` bypasses log-level control, structured JSON output, and any future redaction rules. Replace with `logger.warn({ couponCode }, 'unknown_coupon_code')`.

findings.title: var, ==, and opaque variable names contradict the surrounding TypeScript style
findings.message: The file uses `var x`, `var tmp`, loose `==` equality, and names like `doStuff`, `p`, `amt`, `result2` that carry no semantic meaning. The rest of the codebase exclusively uses `const`/`let`, `===`, and descriptive names. Rename `doStuff` → `applyCouponDiscount`, `tmp` → `strategy`, `p` → `discountRate`, `amt` → `discountAmount`, `result2` → `discountedAmount`, and switch `var` to `const` and `==` to `===` throughout.

findings.title: couponCode is not declared in chargeSchema — no input validation or type safety
findings.message: `couponCode` is read from the raw, unvalidated `req.body` before `chargeSchema.parse()` strips it. It is never validated for type, length, or character set. An attacker can pass an array, object, or an arbitrarily long string. Add `couponCode: z.string().toUpperCase().max(32).optional()` to `chargeSchema` and read it from `payload` post-parse to get proper validation for free.

findings.title: No unit tests for coupon math — the test plan explicitly defers them
findings.message: The PR description marks 'Add unit tests for the coupon math' as a TODO follow-up. Given that the discount calculation is already broken (discount_amount = 0) and corner cases like fixed discounts exceeding the charge amount can cause 500s, tests should be a merge prerequisite, not a follow-up. Add tests covering: each coupon code produces the correct discounted amount, unknown codes are ignored, a fixed discount larger than the amount is handled gracefully, and the response shape when couponCode is omitted.
